title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Also, you know who I'm going to talk about, right?
So we're going to tell the whole story in the next episode.

title: will / shall
examples: Sometimes they'll even have security.
It's kind of odd, but I guess it's convenient and it worked out well for us.
I'll never forget when we stayed in San Francisco.
And then there was snow and I was like, how the hell did this happen?
That will never happen in New York.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Could almost die by sweating so much.
We were able to use the bathrooms there.
We could get food if we needed anything.
But most of the time, we would just park anywhere that we could find.
If you ever go to the national parks in the US, you can get a pass.

title: have to / must
examples: We really had to do this on a budget.
If you like hiking, you must go to Zion.
I have to go to bathroom.
You must do it.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: We would just lay down some blankets, pillows.
We would feed the blankets through the window and roll up the windows so that we had some makeshift curtains for privacy.
But most of the time, we would just park anywhere that we could find.
My dad would have been so proud, though.
On the days where we were really sick of PBJ's, we'd go to Taco Bell and order off their, like, dollar menu.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: I don't think people are allowed to swim there now.
When we went there, you were allowed to swim.
